channel.title: My Customized Papers - Last 24 Hours
channel.description: Research papers from the last 24 hours, filtered by keywords

item.title: [ScienceDirect Publication: Computational Materials Science] Atomistically anchored defect-order kinetics govern threshold renormalization and filament stochasticity in memristive switching
item.description:
Publication date: 25 July 2026

Source: Computational Materials Science, Volume 272

Author(s): Tianyi Chen
item.author: ScienceDirect Publication: Computational Materials Science

item.title: [Nature Communications] Imaging of a van der Waals spin-orbit torque system using spin ensembles in hBN
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74178-7

Recently field-free spin-orbit torque switching has been demonstrated in van der Waals magnetic materials. Here, taking advantage of the quantum sensing properties of spin defects in hexagonal Boron Nitride, Zhang, Zhou, and coauthors image the current-induced spin-orbit torque switching in the van der Waals magnet, Fe3GaTe2
item.author: Nature Communications
